summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of Islamic scholarly website 'فيوض التأويل المعاصر' with focus on new design elements, navigation, and functionality. All major features tested successfully including olive green background, new calligraphy logo, Islamic 8-pointed star, navigation between pages, search functionality, and responsive design.
passed_tests: Homepage loads correctly with olive green gradient background (4 elements with olive green detected)
New logo (logo_calligraphy.png) displays prominently in teardrop shape with Arabic calligraphy
Islamic 8-pointed star (الرُّبع) pattern found in SVG elements - no Star of David patterns detected
Main Arabic title 'فيوض التأويل المعاصر' displays correctly with proper Arabic fonts
Navigation button 'ابدأ التفسير' successfully routes to /part1 page
Part1 page loads and displays Quranic verses with proper RTL text and verse markers (﴿)
Part2 page loads and displays Quranic verses (verses 102-200) with proper formatting
Navigation between Part1 and Part2 works correctly via part navigation tabs
Navigation back to homepage from interpretation pages works
Search functionality works - search input accepts Arabic terms and routes to /part1
Multiple search terms tested (الصبر, التوبة, الرزق) all redirect correctly
Quick access cards (آية اليوم, آيات الرحمة, آيات التوجيه) are clickable and functional
Search section with filter tabs (الكل, آية, سورة, موضوع, كلمة, جذر) displays properly
Gold color scheme implemented correctly (69 elements with gold coloring detected)
Arabic fonts (Amiri, Noto Naskh Arabic) properly applied (843 elements detected)
Responsive design works on tablet (768x1024) and mobile (390x844) viewports
Main title and navigation buttons remain visible and functional on mobile
Language switcher with 5 languages (العربية, English, اردو, Bahasa, Türkçe) works
Geometric Islamic patterns visible in background
Basmala (بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ) displays correctly
Page title shows both Arabic and English: 'فيوض التأويل المعاصر | Fuyud Al-Ta'wil Al-Mu'asir'
